verses.book: Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
verses.text: The custom of the feast bids me write a festal letter, but the cloud of our calamities suffers me not to gather the usual happy fruit from it.

verses.book: Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
verses.text: Who is so stony-hearted as not to be shocked and affrighted at the anger and grief of the Lord?

verses.book: Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
verses.text: Who is not stirred to the memory of faults?

verses.book: Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
verses.text: Who does not look for the righteous sentence?

verses.book: Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
verses.text: All this dims the brightness of the feast, but the Lord is full of loving-kindness, and we trust He will not actually fulfil His threats, but will look mercifully on us, scatter our sadness, open the springs of mercy, and shew His wonted long suffering.

verses.book: Letters of the Blessed Theodoret, Bishop of Cyprus.
verses.text: I salute your greatness, and beseech you to send me news of the health I sincerely trust you are enjoying.
